title: Friday Sermon 1 February 1985
description:
A Review of the Pakistani Government's 'White Paper' Qadiyaniyyat — A Grave Threat to Islam: Was Ahmadiyya Muslim Community Planted By The British? The Truth Revealed in the Light of Historical Facts.

In 1984 the Islamic government of Pakistan set aside all Islamic injunctions and took upon itself the burden of depriving the Ahmadi Muslims of many basic human rights including religious and social freedoms. In an attempt to justify this action, the government of Pakistan published a so-called White Paper under the title 'Qadiyaniyyat - Islam Key Liyey Sangin Khatrah' ['Qadiyaniyyat - A Grave Threat to Islam'].

Although there was nothing new in this so-called White Paper and the Community literature already included detailed answers to all the issues which were raised, nevertheless Hazrat Mirza Tahir Ahmad - Khalifatul Masih IV, the then Imam of the Ahmadiyya Muslim Community, answered these allegations in a series of Friday Sermons.

In this sermon, His Holiness answers the oft-repeated accusation that the Ahmadiyya Muslim Community was created by the British government in the sub-continent of India to serve their purpose and to cause dissension among Muslims. Giving a forceful and effective refutation, he proves in the light of historical facts that the Ahmadiyya Muslim Community was notplanted by the British government. This plant was established by Allah the Almighty with His own Divine hands and He has always safeguarded it and will keep it under His protection in future. If any section could be considered a plant of the British, it could only be the Deoband sect.

The text http://www.alislam.org/urdu/sermon/FST19850201-UR.pdf
English translation http://store.alislam.org/wasahmujaplb.html
